_meta.author: scorecard-refine-local autopilot
_meta.note: LOCAL batch: Austin TX council batch-3 — Harper-Madison (D1), Ellis (D8), Ryan Alter (D5), Fuentes (D2), Qadri (D9). Evidence-only; every TRUE/FALSE cited; consistent with natl-local-tx-austin-2026-06-10-0945 precedents (tax-increase advocacy = econ q2 FALSE; abortion-access sponsorship/endorsement = sanctity FALSE; SB 14 trans-resolution cosponsor/vote = marriage q2+q4 FALSE; public_justice q0 high-bar: routine police-contract yes = null, defund vote or contract no-vote = FALSE; refuse_state_overreach null where local-control fights run in the progressive direction; anti-ICE advocacy = border q1+q2 FALSE). All five web-verified sitting as of June 2026. Source-conflict note: one source (Live Action/KVUE) lists Ellis as a NO on the Aug 2024 abortion-travel amendment while another report calls her a co-sponsor — that contested item is excluded from her citations; her life cells rest on the uncontested GRACE Act co-sponsorship and her direct KUT quote.

profile.background: Austin City Council D1 (east Austin) since Jan 2019; re-elected Nov 2022; former Mayor Pro Tem. East Austin native, small-business background. Term-limited: not on the Nov 2026 ballot; term ends Jan 6, 2027 (D1 is an open seat with candidates already filed). 2019 Ethics Review Commission reprimand for campaign-finance violations; a 2025 ethics complaint (title misuse) was dismissed without a motion.

natasha-harper-madison.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local autopilot, Austin batch-3). Sitting D1 member, web-verified June 2026; term-limited, term ends Jan 2027. Voted for the GRACE Act (July 2022, unanimous) deprioritizing abortion-ban enforcement, remarking 'it's hard to call Texas a pro-life state with any degree of seriousness.' Spoke for the council's transgender-health-care protection resolution (disclosed she has a transgender child) and the intersex-healthcare resolution; promoted Austin Pride on her official Instagram. Authored the June 2020 resolution eliminating funding for new/vacant APD positions during the $150M FY21 police-budget restructuring and voted against accepting a state auto-theft-detective grant. Co-sponsored the 2021 resolution urging Congress to pass a path to citizenship ('should outrage us all'). FY2024 $5.5B budget yes with sponsored spending amendments; her office favored a 5-cent tax-rate-election option in 2025. Public-justice cells beyond q0 (bail, restitution) had no evidence; election-integrity, schools, christian-liberty, gun cells null — no positions found.

0.note: Voted for the GRACE Act (July 2022) deprioritizing abortion-ban enforcement; her own remark at the meeting: 'it's hard to call Texas a pro-life state with any degree of seriousness' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted to decriminalize/deprioritize abortion enforcement citywide — the opposite of abolition advocacy.

0.note: No statement found on the definition of marriage.

2.note: Spoke in support of the council's transgender-health-care protection resolution, disclosing she has a transgender child — affirmation of gender-transition policy, contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Praised the council's intersex-healthcare resolution ('truly inclusive community') and promoted Austin Pride week on her official account — affirmative promotion of LGBTQ-identity policy, not opposition.

0.note: No school-choice or homeschool position found.

1.note: No parental-notification/consent position found.

2.note: No K-12 curriculum positions found (city council has no AISD jurisdiction).

0.note: Religion not publicly stated; no public-faith positions found.

2.note: Voted for the record $5.5B FY2024 budget while sponsoring additional spending amendments (~$400K Carver Museum, mobile court, mental-health facility); in 2025 her office said she favored a 5-cent tax-rate-election option — a pro-spending/tax-increase posture contrary to fiscal restraint.

0.note: No evidence found.

1.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Authored the June 2020 resolution eliminating funding for new officers and unfilled vacant APD positions amid the $150M FY21 police-budget restructuring, and voted against accepting a state grant for auto-theft detectives — documented defund-direction record, the contrary of publicly backing law enforcement.

1.note: No cashless-bail or prosecutor-policy positions found (council role).

3.note: Sponsorship of public-health violence-prevention programs noted, but no rubric-matching statement avoiding punishment of violent crime found.

0.note: No rubric-matching local-control assertion found; her police-cut fight drew state retaliation but ran in the progressive direction.

1.note: No evidence found.

Co-sponsored the 2021 council resolution urging Congress to pass immigration reform with a path to citizenship, saying failure to fix immigration 'should outrage us all' — contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: No specific sanctuary/ICE-cooperation vote or statement attributable to her found (questionnaire answers not retrievable).

0.note: No statement found on permitless carry.

1.note: Violence-prevention program sponsorship is not a rubric-matching gun-restriction position; no direct red-flag/ban statement found.

profile.education: BA Sociology, University of Texas at San Antonio
profile.background: Austin City Council D8 (southwest Austin/Oak Hill) since Jan 2019; re-elected Nov 2022 with 57.8%; Mayor Pro Tem 2023-24. Eight years at an environmental consulting firm. Seeking a rare petition-enabled third term in Nov 2026, challenged by former EMS union president Selena Xie. Received an Ethics Review Commission letter over late return of excess campaign contributions.

paige-ellis.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local autopilot, Austin batch-3). Sitting D8 member, web-verified June 2026; on the Nov 2026 ballot. Co-sponsored the GRACE Act (July 2022): 'This council is committed to and will continue to support our constituents who overwhelmingly agree that people should have access to abortion.' (One opposition-side report listed her as a NO on the Aug 2024 abortion-travel amendment while another called her a co-sponsor — contested, excluded from scoring.) Co-sponsored the intersex-surgery condemnation resolution; on council for the May 2024 SB 14 trans-protections resolution (passed 10-1, she was not the dissenter). Voted for the unanimous Aug 2020 ~$150M APD cut/divert, then the state-compelled 2021 restoration. Co-proposed the 5-cent Prop Q tax-rate increase (rejected by voters Nov 2025) and voted for the $6.3B budget triggering it. Jan 2026 joint statement after an APD-assisted ICE deportation: 'These ICE deportations are wrong.' Co-sponsored 2022 resolutions on gun violence including restricting AR-15 sales to under-21s and a free gun-lock program. Fiscal deviation noted for fairness: publicly called Project Connect's $32M office lease 'the wrong move' (May 2026). Opposed a designated homeless campsite in her district.

0.note: Co-sponsored the GRACE Act deprioritizing abortion-ban enforcement; her own words: 'This council is committed to and will continue to support our constituents who overwhelmingly agree that people should have access to abortion' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Co-sponsored citywide deprioritization of abortion-ban enforcement — the opposite of abolition advocacy.

0.note: No statement found on the definition of marriage.

2.note: On council for the May 2024 SB 14 trans-protections resolution (passed 10-1; she was not the dissenter) and co-sponsored the intersex-healthcare resolution — gender-ideology-affirming record, contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Co-sponsorship and support of LGBTQ-identity-affirming city resolutions — promotion, not opposition.

0.note: No school-choice or homeschool position found.

1.note: No parental-notification/consent position found.

0.note: Religion not publicly stated; no public-faith positions found.

2.note: Co-proposed the 5-cent property-tax increase that became Prop Q (~$300+/yr for the average homeowner; rejected by voters Nov 2025) and voted for the $6.3B budget triggering the tax-rate election — contrary to fiscal restraint. Noted for fairness: she publicly criticized Project Connect's $32M office lease (May 2026).

0.note: No evidence found.

1.note: No evidence found.

0.note: Voted with the unanimous Aug 2020 council to cut/divert ~$150M from APD's $434M budget; the 2021 restoration she later voted for was compelled by state law HB 1900 — the defund vote is the affirmative act on record.

1.note: No cashless-bail or prosecutor-policy positions found.

0.note: Her defiance-of-state items (GRACE Act, SB 14 deprioritization) run in the progressive direction — not rubric-matching local-control credit.

1.note: No evidence found.
Jan 2026 joint statement after an APD-assisted ICE deportation: 'These ICE deportations are wrong' — contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Same statement: 'We share a commitment to doing everything we can to keep immigration enforcement out of the relationship between our constituents and our police' — sanctuary-aligned posture, not opposition to it.

0.note: No statement found on permitless carry.

1.note: Co-sponsored the 2022 resolution seeking to ban/reduce AR-15 sales to under-21s and sponsored the city's gun-lock/safe-storage program — gun-restriction advocacy contrary to this cell.

profile.education: BS Engineering, University of Texas at Austin; JD Harvard Law School
profile.background: Austin City Council D5 (south-central Austin/Zilker) since Jan 2023. Attorney; former Texas Senate staffer (Watson, Garcia, Hinojosa). Married, three children. Running for re-election Nov 2026. Not related to former D10 member Alison Alter.

ryan-alter.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local autopilot, Austin batch-3). Sitting D5 member, web-verified June 2026 (quoted in role May 2026); fundraising for the Nov 2026 ballot. In the 7-vote majority on the Aug 2024 FY2025 abortion-travel-fund amendment (the four no votes were named and he was not among them). Co-sponsored the May 2024 SB 14 trans-protections resolution (first Texas city to deprioritize enforcement). Publicly campaigned for the Prop Q 5-cent tax-rate increase, calling its 63% defeat the loss of a 'lifeline.' On HB 2127 preemption: 'This bill really is a true threat to what cities can do.' Defended Austin's policy limiting ICE cooperation on administrative warrants and signed the 2026 council letter against 'unconstitutional and violent federal operations.' Public-justice cells held to the high bar: his police-contract posture was oversight-conditional (would vote down any contract not implementing the Police Oversight Act) plus a quarterly police-spend transparency resolution — neither affirmative pro-police backing nor defund advocacy → null. Voted yes on HOME zoning density reform (Dec 2023, 9-2). Gun cells null — the red-flag advocacy in Austin coverage belongs to Alison Alter, not him.

0.note: In the 7-vote majority for the Aug 2024 FY2025 budget amendment funding $500K in out-of-state abortion travel (the four dissenters were named and he was not among them) — abortion-access support contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Voted to fund out-of-state abortion travel — the opposite of abolition advocacy.

0.note: No statement found on the definition of marriage.

2.note: Co-sponsored the May 2024 resolution deprioritizing enforcement of SB 14 (ban on gender-transition treatments for minors) and barring city funds from related investigations — contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Same cosponsorship — affirmative promotion of gender-identity policy, not opposition.

0.note: No school-choice or homeschool position found.

1.note: No parental-notification/consent position found.

0.note: Religion not publicly stated; no public-faith positions found.

2.note: Publicly campaigned for Prop Q, the Nov 2025 5-cent property-tax-rate increase (~$110M/yr); after its 63% rejection called the failure the loss of a 'lifeline' — tax-increase advocacy contrary to fiscal restraint.

0.note: No evidence found.

1.note: No evidence found.

0.note: High-bar rule: his police-contract posture was oversight-conditional (said he would vote down any contract not fully implementing the Police Oversight Act) plus a quarterly spend-transparency resolution — neither affirmative pro-police advocacy nor defund advocacy.

1.note: No cashless-bail or prosecutor-policy positions found.

0.note: His HB 2127 opposition ('a true threat to what cities can do') is a local-control fight in the progressive direction — not rubric-matching.

Defended Austin's policy limiting ICE cooperation: residents won't 'be scooped up by ICE' on administrative warrants — contrary to supporting mandatory deportation.

2.note: Signed the council letter on 'protecting the people of Austin from unconstitutional and violent federal operations' and defended limited-ICE-cooperation policy — sanctuary-aligned posture, not opposition to it.

profile.education: BS Public Relations, University of Texas at Austin; executive master's, LBJ School of Public Affairs
profile.background: Austin City Council D2 (southeast Austin/Del Valle) since Jan 2021; re-elected Nov 2024 with ~86% over Ryan Reynolds; elected Mayor Pro Tem Jan 2025. Raised in Brady, TX; first-generation college graduate. Next D2 election 2028.

vanessa-fuentes.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local autopilot, Austin batch-3). Sitting D2 member and Mayor Pro Tem, web-verified June 2026; no evidence of seeking another office. Co-proposed the GRACE Act (July 2022) deprioritizing abortion-ban enforcement; led creation of the $400K abortion-travel Reproductive Justice Fund and authored the Aug 2024 'reproductive rights are human rights' resolution (10-1); called the fund's SB 33-forced end a 'disappointment.' Co-sponsored and voted for the May 2024 SB 14 trans-protections resolution. Among four members proposing a 6.75-cent tax-rate increase in 2025 (Prop Q rejected by voters). Supported Aug 2021 mask mandates in defiance of Abbott's order banning them. Strong anti-preemption ('The Death Star Law is... a calculated tool to control local governments') and anti-ICE posture (2026 council letter, Austin Chronicle op-ed action plan, said city management 'capitulated to the Governor's unreasonable demand' on APD ICE policy). She did NOT vote on the Aug 2020 police cut (took office Jan 2021). Public-justice q0 held to the high bar: her yes on the Oct 2024 APA contract (10-1) was framed around 'accountability and transparency' — routine governance plus oversight framing, not affirmative pro-police advocacy → null. Moderating data point noted for fairness: that police-contract yes vote despite progressive-bloc membership.

0.note: Co-proposed the GRACE Act and led the $400K abortion-travel fund, authoring the Aug 2024 resolution declaring 'reproductive rights are human rights' and calling the fund a 'vital resource' — contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Architect of city-funded out-of-state abortion travel; called its forced end a 'disappointment' — the opposite of abolition advocacy.

0.note: No statement found on the definition of marriage.

2.note: Co-sponsored and voted for the May 2024 resolution deprioritizing SB 14 enforcement and directing city support for 'gender-affirming care'; joined the post-vote press conference supporting trans Austinites — contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.

4.note: Same cosponsorship and advocacy — affirmative promotion of gender-identity policy, not opposition.

0.note: Religion not publicly stated; no public-faith positions found.

2.note: Among four council members proposing a 6.75-cent property-tax-rate increase in 2025 budget talks (council set the rate triggering the Prop Q election, the largest increase in city history; voters rejected it) — tax-increase advocacy contrary to fiscal restraint.

0.note: No evidence found.

1.note: No evidence found.

0.note: High-bar rule: voted yes (10-1) on the Oct 2024 APA contract but framed support around 'accountability and transparency' and questioned costs — routine governance with oversight framing, not affirmative pro-police advocacy. She did NOT vote on the Aug 2020 police cut (took office Jan 2021).

1.note: No cashless-bail or prosecutor-policy positions found.

2.note: Supported Aug 2021 mask mandates ('we stand in solidarity with the city and county decision to require masks') — advocacy for imposing mandates on citizens, the contrary of protecting citizens from lockdowns/mandates.

profile.education: MPA and MA (graduate degrees in public administration and political science)
profile.background: Austin City Council D9 (central Austin incl. UT campus) since Jan 2023; first Muslim elected to the Austin City Council. Former advocacy-firm staffer. 2022 endorsements included Vote Pro Choice, Emgage PAC, Elizabeth Warren, and James Talarico. Running for re-election Nov 2026. Joined the Dec 2023 call (with Fuentes, Velásquez) for an Israel-Hamas ceasefire, drawing Abbott funding threats.

zohaib-zo-qadri.notes_set: Evidence-finalized 2026-06-10 (local autopilot, Austin batch-3). Sitting D9 member, web-verified June 2026 (quoted in role Jan and May 2026); fundraising for the Nov 2026 ballot. Endorsed by Vote Pro Choice (2022, single-issue endorsement) and previously worked at an advocacy firm combating anti-abortion legislation. Co-sponsored the May 2024 SB 14 trans-protections resolution. Sole NO vote on the Oct 2024 five-year $218M Austin Police Association contract: 'I don't believe this contract reflects the values of the majority of Austinites'; said it 'prioritizes the police department from all other city services.' Co-proposed the 5-cent Prop Q tax-rate increase and voted for the $6.3B budget triggering the election (voters rejected Prop Q Nov 2025). Anti-ICE record: supported the 'Safe to Call' resolution limiting arrests of undocumented 911 callers; said no one on council 'wants to see ICE in Austin.' Opposes HB 2127 preemption (progressive direction → overreach cells null). Conservative-coded data point noted for fairness: yes on the HOME zoning density deregulation (Dec 2023, 9-2). Election-integrity cells null — pre-office 'voter suppression' advocacy work was too vague to score. Gun cells null — no evidence found.

0.note: Endorsed by Vote Pro Choice in 2022 — a single-issue abortion-rights endorsement, hard evidence contrary to affirming personhood from conception.

1.note: Vote Pro Choice endorsement plus prior professional work combating anti-abortion legislation — the opposite of abolition advocacy.

2.note: Co-sponsored the May 2024 Transgender Protection Resolution deprioritizing SB 14 enforcement and barring city funds from trans-healthcare investigations — contrary to affirming biological sex as immutable.
